items.title: USDA finalizes new SNAP retailer stocking standards (7 varieties per category)
items.summary: USDA published a final rule updating SNAP retailer stocking standards, requiring most SNAP-authorized retailers to stock at least 7 varieties in each of the 4 staple food categories (dairy, fruits/vegetables, grains, protein). Retailers must also offer a perishable variety in at least 3 of the 4 categories, with compliance required by Nov. 4, 2026.

items.title: Montana submits SNAP waiver request to restrict soft drinks, junk food, and candy
items.summary: USDA announced that Montana submitted a waiver request seeking to ban soft drinks, junk food, and candy purchases using SNAP benefits. USDA said it expects state waivers to go into effect in 2026, part of a broader initiative encouraging states to propose changes to federal nutrition programs.

items.title: SUN Bucks (Summer EBT) provides $120 per eligible child for summer groceries
items.summary: USDA’s Summer EBT (SUN Bucks) program provides $120 in grocery benefits per eligible school-age child when school is out for summer. Many children get benefits automatically if their household already participates in SNAP, FDPIR, or TANF; others may need to apply depending on their state or Tribal program.

items.title: CMS proposes extending electronic prior authorization requirements to prescription drugs (comments due June 15, 2026)
items.summary: CMS released a proposed rule to extend electronic prior authorization requirements to cover drugs, aiming for faster and more transparent decisions. CMS says the proposal would require impacted payers to support electronic prior authorization and report API endpoints and usage metrics; public comments are due June 15, 2026.

items.title: HUD sets Jan. 1, 2027 enforcement date for HOTMA income/asset changes; PHAs may need to start Sept. 2026
items.summary: HUD issued Notice PIH 2026-15 stating it will enforce compliance with HOTMA sections 102 and 104 starting Jan. 1, 2027 for most public housing agencies, affecting programs like Housing Choice Vouchers and public housing. HUD notes PHAs that start annual reexams 120 days ahead may need to begin using HOTMA policies to collect and verify income beginning in September 2026.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: SSA watchdog finds estimated $50.4M underpaid to 8,618 widow(er)s; 41% of sample had issues
items.summary: SSA’s Office of Inspector General reported that 59% of sampled widow(er) beneficiaries were paid correctly, while 41% were paid inappropriately or lacked documentation that SSA employees explained filing options. The audit estimated SSA underpaid 8,618 widow(er)s by about $50.4 million and recommended calculation and documentation fixes.

items.title: IRS extends Washington state disaster tax relief deadlines to Aug. 5, 2026
items.summary: The IRS updated its Washington state disaster tax relief notice to postpone various filing and payment deadlines to Aug. 5, 2026 for taxpayers affected by severe storms, flooding, landslides, and mudslides that began Dec. 9, 2025. The postponement includes individual returns and payments, estimated tax payments (including Jan. 15 and April 15, 2026 installments), and certain quarterly payroll and excise tax returns.

items.title: Education Department guidance reflects new 'Workforce Pell' eligibility for some short-term training programs
items.summary: In its May 2026 COD Technical Reference updates for 2026–27, the U.S. Department of Education describes legislative changes that extend Pell Grant eligibility to a student enrolled in an eligible workforce program, even if the student already has a bachelor’s degree. The same guidance notes an ineligibility threshold for Pell when a student’s SAI is at least twice the maximum Pell award for the year, stating the 2026–27 threshold as $14,790.

items.title: IRS details new 1% excise tax on certain remittance transfers; first deposit due Jan. 29, 2026
items.summary: The IRS says a new 1% excise tax applies to certain remittance transfers beginning Jan. 1, 2026 when the sender pays with cash or similar physical instruments (like money orders or cashier’s checks). Remittance transfer providers must make semimonthly deposits and file quarterly returns, with the first semimonthly deposit due Jan. 29, 2026.
